channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Sonia Gandhi under medical observation for minor health issue
item.description: Senior Congress leader Sonia Gandhi was admitted to a hospital in New Delhi as a precautionary measure after she experienced mild health-related discomfort. According to doctors familiar with the case...

item.title: Residents in Alandur still lack basic civic facilities despite new infrastructure projects
item.description: In the run-up to the 2026 Tamil Nadu Assembly elections, the Alandur constituency stands out as an area where visible infrastructure projects coexist with long-standing gaps in basic civic amenities. ...

item.title: Government Considers Adjusting Fuel Prices Amid Soaring Global Rates
item.description: Amid a sharp increase in global oil prices, Pakistan’s government is contemplating to end the freeze on petrol and diesel prices while introducing targeted subsidies for two- and three-wheelers. Jet f...

item.title: Gujarat becomes second Indian state to enact Uniform Civil Code after Uttarakhand
item.description: The Gujarat Legislative Assembly has passed the Uniform Civil Code (UCC) Bill, making Gujarat the second Indian state, after Uttarakhand, to implement such a law. The Bill seeks to bring uniformity in...

item.title: Libya's Sandton Property Investments Fail to Benefit Citizens Over Two Decades
item.description: Over the past two decades, Libya's $210-million investment in real estate in Sandton, Johannesburg, including the iconic Michelangelo Hotel, has failed to deliver any tangible benefits for the Libyan ...

item.title: RCMP investigates corruption allegations involving Calgary city officials and executes search warrants
item.description: The RCMP has launched a corruption investigation involving several current and former Calgary city officials. Search warrants and production orders were executed last week at multiple properties, incl...

item.title: AFL Faces Expanding Concussion Class Action with 10 Additional Clubs Named
item.description: The ongoing concussion-related class action against the AFL has grown, with ten more clubs now listed as defendants. The legal action, originally led by former Geelong player Max Rooke, addresses clai...

item.title: American Woman Shares Her Joyful Life with Wealthy Nigerian Yoruba Chief Husband
item.description: Shann, an American woman living in Nigeria with her Yoruba chief husband, has shared her experience of being pampered by him. She revealed that her husband spoils her daily, buying whatever she wants,...

item.title: Review of 'Power: The Downfall of Huw Edwards' – A Disturbing Portrayal of a Grooming Scandal
item.description: Channel 5’s drama 'Power: The Downfall of Huw Edwards' dramatises the fall of the BBC newsreader who resigned after being accused of making indecent images of children. Martin Clunes plays Edwards in ...

item.title: Why Authoritarian Leaders Build Personality Cults Around Themselves
item.description: The article by Keith B. Richburg explores the phenomenon of personality cults among authoritarian leaders and the insecurities that drive them to display their own images everywhere. It begins with No...

item.title: Kerala Election Authorities Clear V.D. Satheesan’s Nomination Despite LDF Allegations
item.description: In the run-up to Kerala's 2026 Assembly elections, opposition leader V.D. Satheesan's nomination papers were accepted by the authorities, dismissing the Left Democratic Front’s (LDF) allegations. The ...

item.title: Iran’s Conditions for a Cease-Fire in Ongoing Middle East Conflict
item.description: According to The Wall Street Journal’s live coverage, Iran has presented a firm and ambitious list of demands in ongoing cease-fire negotiations related to the escalating Middle East conflict involvin...

item.title: IGP Redeploys Delta Police Commissioner Amid Internal Conflicts and Festival Controversy
item.description: The Inspector-General of Police (IGP), Tunji Disu, has redeployed Aina Adesola, the Commissioner of Police in Delta State, less than three months after his appointment on January 5, 2026. Adesola had ...

item.title: Government Issues Directive to Boost Piped Natural Gas Infrastructure
item.description: The Indian government has issued an important directive aimed at strengthening the country’s piped natural gas (PNG) infrastructure. This move is expected to enhance the accessibility and delivery of ...

item.title: The Global Challenge of Tuberculosis and the Need for an Effective Vaccine
item.description: Tuberculosis (TB) remains a major health issue, particularly in low-income countries where poverty and overcrowded living conditions facilitate its spread. Despite being rare in high-income countries ...

item.title: House Committee Confirms Sufficiency of Two Impeachment Complaints Against VP Sara Duterte
item.description: On March 25, 2026, the House Committee on Justice evaluated two impeachment complaints filed against Vice President Sara Duterte. After careful review, the committee determined that both complaints we...

item.title: Emily Gregory wins Florida state special election, flipping seat near Trump’s Mar-a-Lago
item.description: The Democratic Party secured a notable victory in Florida after winning a special election for a state legislature seat that includes the area surrounding former President Donald Trump’s Mar-a-Lago es...

item.title: Singer Asa Mourns the Death of Her Mother After Sudden Illness
item.description: Nigerian music star Bukola Elemide, wey people sabi as Asa, don announce say her mama don die after she battle small time with brain tumour. The 43-year-old singer share the sad tori on her Instagram ...

item.title: DMK Finalises Seat-Sharing with Allies for Tamil Nadu Assembly Polls 2026, DMDK Gets 10 Seats
item.description: In a major development ahead of the Tamil Nadu Assembly elections scheduled for 2026, the ruling Dravida Munnetra Kazhagam (DMK) has almost completed seat-sharing arrangements within the Secular Progr...

item.title: Sara Duterte Impeachment Hearing Continues Despite Absence
item.description: The House of Representatives’ justice committee proceeded with the formal impeachment hearing of Vice President Sara Duterte on March 25, 2026, despite her legal team's notice of non-attendance. The p...
